$comment: SEAM-BOUNDARY SCOPE (RFUP item 1 / phase-2-pediatric-schema-gate, P2-001/P2-003): this schema enforces STRUCTURAL COMPLETENESS ONLY for the pediatric_cds evidence-card extension block -- required-field presence and JSON type. It does NOT encode clinical business rules or semantics owned by pediatric-anemia-site: no enum constraints on portability/assertion_kind/evidence_role/classification vocab, no age-partition or lab-method-analyzer validity rules, no threshold-value interpretation, no numeric clinical bounds (e.g. sensitivity/specificity ranges). rf validates that whatever pediatric_cds block is supplied is structurally well-formed; it never originates or interprets the block's clinical content. A source card entirely lacking a pediatric_cds block is not itself a violation of this schema -- callers of this schema (rf verify, P2-002) decide whether/when to invoke it, and only invoke it against blocks that ARE present. TWO VARIANTS (P2-003 finding): the 7 existing verified pediatric-CDS bundles (committed aaa9d92, produced by .claude/workflows/rf-pediatric-cds-run-execute.js's CARD_TEMPLATE) use a flat population/assay_method/threshold/lifecycle/classification shape that predates and diverges entirely from the richer 9-section shape named in the pediatric-anemia-site scope-brief (.claude/worknotes/rfup-external-routing/scope-brief.md) and design spec (pediatric-anemia-site docs/project_plans/expansion/02-evidence-foundry-on-research-foundry.md §3.6-3.7). AC-P2-10 requires 0 false positives against those 7 bundles, so this schema accepts EITHER shape via `oneOf` rather than breaking the shape already on disk -- see PediatricCdsBlockLegacy vs PediatricCdsBlockRich below.
title: PediatricCdsBlock
description: JSON Schema for the `pediatric_cds` evidence-card extension block attached to `extracted_points[]` entries on an rf source card. Replaces the prior `additionalProperties: true` permissiveness for this namespace (see PRD FR-3, docs/project_plans/PRDs/enhancements/rfup-external-routing-v1.md) with required-field/type enforcement, accepting either the legacy flat shape already in production (PediatricCdsBlockLegacy) or the richer 9-section target shape from the pediatric-anemia-site design spec (PediatricCdsBlockRich).
x-rf-schema-version-note: This schema artifact's own version stamp reuses the RFUP-4 machine-contract versioning convention verbatim (RF_SCHEMA_VERSION, src/research_foundry/__init__.py:23; see docs/dev/architecture/machine-surface-inventory.md) -- bump alongside RF_SCHEMA_VERSION if this schema's shape changes in a breaking way. Bumped 1.0.0 -> 1.1.0 (P2-003): additive only (the rich variant is unchanged; a legacy variant was added alongside it), so no existing valid rich block stops validating. Distinct from the `schema_version` property inside PediatricCdsBlockRich, which is the pediatric_cds block's OWN shape-version field, authored and owned by pediatric-anemia-site (docs/project_plans/expansion/02-evidence-foundry-on-research-foundry.md §3.6 example: schema_version: "1.0").

PediatricCdsBlockLegacy.title: PediatricCdsBlockLegacy
PediatricCdsBlockLegacy.description: The flat pediatric_cds shape actually produced today by .claude/workflows/rf-pediatric-cds-run-execute.js's CARD_TEMPLATE and present on every extracted_points[].pediatric_cds block in the 7 existing verified pediatric-CDS bundles (aaa9d92). Grandfathered by AC-P2-10 -- this is NOT the aspirational scope-brief shape (that's PediatricCdsBlockRich below); it is the shape that already exists on disk and must keep validating.

population.description: Age band + sex + gestational/postnatal age + clinical context, or 'not applicable'. Free-form; owned by pediatric-anemia-site.

assay_method.description: Analyzer/method dependence for a numeric threshold, or 'not method-dependent' / 'not applicable'. Free-form.

threshold.description: Numeric threshold value + units + exact-passage locator, when applicable.

value.description: Verbatim numeric value + comparator (e.g. "< 11.0"), or null when not applicable.

passage_locator.description: Page/section + the verbatim quote fragment carrying the number, or null.

classification.description: Free-form classification label (e.g. source_supported_fact / implementation_proposal). Vocabulary owned by pediatric-anemia-site; rf enforces type only, not an enum.

PediatricCdsBlockRich.title: PediatricCdsBlockRich
PediatricCdsBlockRich.description: The richer 9-section target shape named in the pediatric-anemia-site scope-brief and design spec (§3.6-3.7) -- required-field/type enforcement across source_status, study, applicability, laboratory, implementable_statement, diagnostic_accuracy, safety, conflict, and lifecycle.

schema_version.description: pediatric_cds block's own shape-version, authored/owned by pediatric-anemia-site (e.g. "1.0"). Not RF_SCHEMA_VERSION -- see the x-rf-schema-version-note above.

module_id.description: Identifier of the pediatric-anemia-site CDS module this evidence point belongs to (e.g. "cbc_suite_v1"). Opaque to rf.

evidence_role.description: Free-form role label for this evidence point (e.g. "threshold"). Vocabulary is owned by pediatric-anemia-site; rf enforces type only, not an enum.

SourceStatus.description: Update/correction/retraction/withdrawal/supersession tracking for the cited source (design doc §3.6, §3.7 'source status' row).

Study.description: Study/guideline design metadata backing this evidence point (design doc §3.6).

Applicability.description: Age/sex/physiology/comorbidity/jurisdiction qualifiers (design doc §3.7: 'population/applicability ... MUST not be dropped').

Laboratory.description: Test/specimen/method/analyzer/unit/reference-interval/timing context (design doc §3.7: 'laboratory context ... MUST be explicit when relevant'). rf does not validate method/analyzer clinical validity, only field presence and type.

ImplementableStatement.description: The executable-rule-candidate statement itself (design doc §3.6). `portability` and `assertion_kind` are typed as free-form strings, not enums, per the seam boundary -- their controlled-vocabulary values (e.g. universal/local_lab_dependent/implementation_proposed) are clinical/business semantics owned by pediatric-anemia-site, not encoded here.

DiagnosticAccuracy.description: Diagnostic-accuracy statistics for this evidence point, when applicable (design doc §3.6). All fields nullable -- not every evidence point carries diagnostic-accuracy data. No numeric bounds enforced (structural type-check only).

Conflict.description: Cross-claim conflict tracking (design doc §3.7: 'conflicts ... MUST be visible'). `safe_representation` is a free-form string, not an enum -- its controlled vocabulary is owned by pediatric-anemia-site.

Lifecycle.description: Review/surveillance metadata (design doc §3.7: 'lifecycle ... MUST be present for implementable clinical content').
